rationale: JARVIS represents the pinnacle of AI interfaces. We are leveraging Archetype 7 for its high-contrast, glowing neon terminal aesthetic. Deep slate/obsidian bases are paired with precise cyan/blue primary lighting and amber warnings. The design language relies on 'Retro-Futurism' mixed with 'Grid Borders' — prioritizing data density, command-center focus, asymmetric tension, and technical precision over soft consumer styling.

spacing_and_layout.grid_strategy: MODE B: THE 'CONTROL ROOM' GRID. Extreme density. Base grid: grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-8 lg:grid-cols-12 gap-4.
spacing_and_layout.widget_styling: Flat backgrounds, strict 1px border (border-cyan-900/50), no heavy box-shadows, subtle inner glow or drop-shadow on text/SVGs. rounded-none.

surface_strategies.name: Glassmorphism (The 'Crystal' HUD)
surface_strategies.application: Floating Bottom Nav Dock, Overlays, Modals

motion_and_interactions.special_effects: Scanlines (repeating-linear-gradient overlay), pulsing glows (animate-pulse) for the Arc Reactor core, typing effects for AI responses.

biometric_login.description: Biometric face scan interface. Digital mesh head background with a vertical scanning laser line overlay.
biometric_login.classes: min-h-screen flex items-center justify-center bg-slate-950 relative overflow-hidden

arc_reactor.description: Central visual anchor of the dashboard. Glowing concentric SVG circles. Rotating animations (slow spin, reverse spin on inner rings).

system_diagnostics.description: Dense data clusters. Circular gauges and line charts for CPU/RAM/Disk. Minimalist line charts.

ai_command_terminal.description: The JARVIS command prompt. Blinking cursor '_', scrolling history of commands, voice I/O visualizer (audio waveform bars).
ai_command_terminal.classes: bg-black/90 font-mono text-cyan-400 p-4 border border-cyan-800

developer_workspace.description: Integrated IDE/coding assistant panel. Syntax highlighting, file tree, Copilot-like suggestion blocks.

neural_sensor_camera.description: Live feed or simulated camera view. Overlay with targeting brackets '[ ]' and scrolling hex codes.
neural_sensor_camera.classes: relative overflow-hidden group grayscale sepia hue-rotate-[180deg]

weather_and_calendar.description: Satellite view background for weather, integrated with a stark, data-driven calendar.

bottom_navigation_dock.description: Central floating dock at the bottom of the viewport for quick module switching.
bottom_navigation_dock.classes: fixed bottom-6 left-1/2 -translate-x-1/2 flex gap-4 px-6 py-3

camera_feed_bg.description: Cyberpunk night street for the neural sensor camera background.

biometric_mesh.description: Wire sculpture of a human head, used for the biometric face scan overlay.

abstract_hologram.description: Abstract blue/cyan holographic swirl to use as ambient background or behind the arc reactor.

satellite_view.description: Earth from space at night with glowing city lights, used for the weather/geo-widget.

libraries.icons: yarn add @phosphor-icons/react (Using tech/geometry-focused icons like TerminalWindow, Cpu, Database, Activity, Eye, Scan)
libraries.charts: recharts (styled strictly with cyan strokes and transparent fills, no soft gradients)
libraries.animations: framer-motion (crucial for HUD pop-ins, scanning laser logic, typing effects, and arc reactor spin)

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. Layout & Grid: Implement a massive 'Control Room' bento grid. The dashboard should ideally be full height (h-screen) with overflow handled inside specific panels (like the terminal or activity feed). No large white space. Every panel should have a 1px solid border (`border-cyan-900/50`).

2. Typography: All text must be highly legible and technical. Import and use 'JetBrains Mono' for data/terminal outputs and 'Azeret Mono' for component headers. Do not use generic sans-serif fonts (like Inter).

3. Arc Reactor: Build this as a complex SVG element centrally located. It MUST use Framer Motion or CSS animations to continuously rotate concentric rings at different speeds/directions. Apply heavy drop-shadows to simulate a glowing light source.

4. Neural Sensor Camera: Use the 'camera_feed_bg' image, scaled with `object-cover`. Add a `mix-blend-luminosity` and `opacity-50` overlay of cyan (`bg-cyan-900/30`). Render absolute positioned SVG corner crosshairs and a blinking red 'REC / ANALYZING' badge.
5. Weather & Calendar: Use the 'satellite_view' image for the weather widget backdrop with a heavy dark overlay. The calendar should be a dense data-grid of days, not a standard soft SaaS calendar.

6. Biometric Login View: Create a dedicated route/view for this. Use the 'biometric_mesh' image centered. Implement a horizontal scanning laser line (a 1px cyan div with heavy glow) that animates vertically across the face using Framer Motion.
7. Edge Styling: Do NOT use rounded corners. Enforce `rounded-none` or at most `rounded-sm` (2px) to maintain the military/tech hardware HUD feel.
8. Charting: Use Recharts for the system diagnostics. Style the Line/Area charts purely with thin colored lines (`stroke='#06b6d4'`, `strokeWidth={1}`), no thick bars. Add raw data point labels.

9. Interaction: Add `data-testid` attributes to all interactive elements. Ensure hover states on buttons/links use inverted styling (cyan background, slate text) for the classic terminal selection look.
10. Navigation: The 'Bottom Navigation Dock' should be fixed, using the 'Glassmorphism' surface strategy (backdrop-blur-2xl) to overlap the grid.
